author: @Rebecka0001
text:
Tiffany, just wanted to say that we’re all here supporting you no matter what. I can only imagine how difficult it’s been navigating the grief of losing your mom. Your family is filled with so much love, and it really shows.

One thing I admire is that even though you loved living in Maui and wanted to stay there, you chose to do what was best for your kids. That says so much about the kind of mother you are and how much you care about their happiness, opinions, and well-being.

At the same time, please remember to take care of yourself too. You don’t always have to be strong or show the best version of yourself. We’re all human, and everyone goes through hard seasons in life. It’s okay to have difficult moments.

I truly love this family, and I hope you realize how much of a positive impact you have on so many of us who watch your videos. Thank you for choosing to share your life with us on YouTube. Sending you all so much love. ❤️
video_title: Last Day Of School Morning Routine *WE'RE MOViNG* 🎒🌴

author: @cyndy7826
text: Thank you for loving HER !! It makes be so happy to see a younger woman cherish what true cooking was all about, from previous generations, and to have the desire to make the recipes ! Those were the days of whole foods, often direct from the farm, to local grocery stores. Women actually cooked all the meals every day and did not worry about added preservatives, hormones, or chemicals etc. We even had our own vegetable gardens. We were healthier: mentally, emotionally, and physically. I am a healthy 71 year old and had that very cookbook handed down to me from my mother; I learned how to cook, garden, and sew from my grandmother, mother, and aunts. While taking college prep classes, I took the local Betty Crocker Award contest, on a whim, and won; I had never taken a home economics class in school. I became a college educated professional and worked 44 years, while raising a family, but still used my Betty Crocker life skills, with gratitude, for the women who taught me the joy and fulfillment of gathering family and friends around a table to share, not only a meal, but the beauty and peace of human connection. I always included my three sons in meal preparation. I am teaching my granddaughter, including my grandsons, my Betty Crocker skills and it brings all of us so much joy to share these moments together. May the loving and purposeful energy in this “treasured” cookbook bless you and future generations; the previous owner may be smiling down on you.
video_title: Went Back for the Cookbook!

author: @CrazyDiamondShiningOn
text:
I just happened upon your video and didn't know what you were looking for until you picked it up. It actually made me cry. My grandmother had hers. My mother had hers and I had the one that my mother gifted me. I lived with my mom and took care of my mom. After she died my younger brother wanted the house sold. Locks ended up being changed and a special lock box was put on the front and back door. 

My mother's 73 years worth of her life was in that house. My 52 years worth of my life was in that house as were my children's things, baby clothes, family mementos, my mother's christening gown from 1948. My baby books, my kids baby books, Christmas ornaments, so many things. I had my original clothes from the 80s in there. I had my little dolls from the end of the 60s in the beginning of the 70s in there. My life was in that house. When my grandma died her cookbook was given to me. I walked out of that house with nothing but the clothes on my back. Now I don't have my grandmother's, my mother's on my own cookbook. My mom wrote a special message in there for me. It was as good as losing everything to a fire. All because the baby of the family wanted to and I quote, "Benefit from mom's death too." (Yeah I know I thought the same thing too. That evil little POS) He was so used to having things handed to him. He never worked more than a year in his life. He conveniently had two car accidents so he could go on disability. He hooked up with someone that was my age, 10+ years older than him. She worked for the government so she supported his butt. 

I on the other hand worked hard as a single mom and pulled myself up from the bottom and raised three kids and took care of my mom and shared the house payment and all the bills with her. But because my mom didn't die with a will, whatever she had would be left to her three kids after her debts were paid off. I continued to pay the mortgage on that home all by myself for 6 months before he pulled his crap. 

I'm sorry to get this out here but maybe I just needed to get it out. It hurts the most when it's the people you love the most who betray you. Mentally I was not well for a long time and I'm still not myself, not just because of this. There's so much more to it and that's another part that really hurts. I've already told you how I pulled myself up but that wasn't all I pulled myself up from. I fought breast cancer and I struggled but I did it.  In addition to that they needed to remove my ovaries and my uterus because of cysts and fibroids and complications there. Without my ovaries, I didn't have my hormones. I couldn't have hormone therapy because that would put me at a higher risk of having my breast cancer come back. The medications I had to take for cancer had an effect on my bones and I have severe arthritis all over my body. The removal of my ovaries threw me into an instant depression and it actually took me out of work where I had a huge nervous breakdown. Talk about embarrassing. I was a strong, determined, responsible mother before that. I had my head held high. I was a good mom and I know it. That was a good daughter. I was faithful and loyal to my family and the Lord. 

You think you know what depression is, but unless you've faced it yourself with the chemical imbalances in your body, you have no clue, absolutely no clue what depression is. I was suffering depression and I didn't know what it was. I hid in my room for 6 months before I called my doctor crying because I couldn't get out of bed and I couldn't leave my room and I couldn't go to work and I kept calling into work and I didn't know what was wrong with me. That was when he explained it to me. 

I'm not saying any of this for any pity I don't want anybody feeling sorry for me. God gave me trials to go through and I went through them. God took care of me. Jesus Christ was with me every step of the way. These were all learning experiences. My mom said they were meant to make me stronger than I was. My mom was very wise, like many are.

Both of my brothers knew exactly what was going on every step of the way in my life and my mother's life. Yet they both managed to stay away from myself and my mother. One brother walked away from everything and didn't want anything to do with anyone after that. And then there was the greedy one. He didn't give two rats asses if I was homeless and had to go and sleep on my best friend's couch, my daughter moved in with her friend, then I had to move in with her and her friend before we could get me situated in an apartment. I was lucky I had to support any encouragement that I did. My friends and my three kids were going to let me fall like that. I got through it and I'm thankful I had them and the Lord with me.

I wanted to move back to my home state, but all of my family are now gone. All I have left in the world are my three children. So I have to stay here in the state they live in, because other than that, I have no one. 

I wrestled with my ghosts and there's a lot from the past that haunts me. I have had to learn to let go. In a way I pretend like none of that ever happened. I can't think about the things that I lost because it hurts. I don't have any of the things to pass down to my daughter. I can't give her the Hope chest that my mother gave me. My dad bought it for my mom when they got married way back in 1967. All the family recipes from generations that were written down. My grandmother came to the US when she was 2 years old. She traveled here on a ship with her mom and siblings from Hungary. I have no photographs of the family. I don't have any pictures of My grandparents for my father or my mother or when they were little. My children's baby pictures are all gone. All the research on the family tree that I did for 22 years written in multiple notebooks in addition to many many books I purchased that backed up all of my information for our genealogy. 

I have to let it go. I did for the most part but it's still there. Every so often it comes up and taps me on the shoulder like this book did. If anybody does take the time to read this I thank you. I'm sorry if I brought you down. There was only one thing that I lost that meant the most to me and I hope someday I see her again when Jesus takes me home. Oh what I wouldn't give just to hug my mom one more time.

I keep telling myself it's all just stuff. Yoko once said to John, "When you're dead you can't take nothing with you but your soul." He took her words and put them in a song. That line from that song has always stuck with me. Something I need to keep reminding myself. It was just stuff. Things. The important things are the things that we CAN take with us. Our love of our family and our love, faith and trust in our Lord Jesus Christ. I could be the last person standing on the Earth, but I would never be alone because I have Him.

If you did read all of this I thank you. Love your family. Love your children. Thank the Lord everyday for everything he has given you good or bad. It was for a reason. He needed you to learn something and he knew you could handle it just like I did. I still struggle. Clearly. But now that I've had my little mini breakdown and I was able to get some of this out, it's time to pick myself up and move on again. My mom always told me that God never gives us more than we can handle. 

There was another thing my mom used to always say to me. I had forgotten about it. But after she died I was standing in her room and for some reason a piece of paper just fell off of her bed and onto the floor in front of me. It was strange because there were no fans on and no windows open. I picked up that piece of paper. She had written down what she had told me so many times before and it fell on the floor in front of me. I felt like she was saying it again. So before I tell you what it was, I will say thank you. Praise God everyday for the wonderful gift of life and it's many lessons. Say a prayer for someone else. There are many people out there that are still struggling and need prayers. We can't all be everywhere at once. But no matter where we are we can talk to God at any time. 

My final gift from my mom: 

Don't worry about anything, instead pray about everything. Tell God your needs and don't forget to thank him for His answers. If you do this you will experience God's peace, which is far more wonderful than the human mind can understand. His peace will keep your thoughts and your hearts quiet and at rest as you trust in Christ Jesus.    Philippians 4:6

God bless you

text: My grandmother passed away this past January...she was 91. If there was one thing that I never forgot, was her recipe for cornbread. She would take the cornbread, bake it in the oven in a seasoned cast-iron skillet, and then crisp up the edges on the stove. It was THE best cornbread I've ever had. When she passed, one of my younger brothers, our father, and my husband went to handle her affairs. They brought back her cook books and recipes for me. As I was flipping through one of the cook books (one she cooked in religiously for decades), tucked into one of the many pages, was her cornbread recipe. THE cornbread recipe. I cried upon seeing it. It meant more to me than anything else. My heart is full and my soul is happy. <3

author: @lanaserenelife
text:
Hello all :)

About to go to sleep for the first time in over 35 hours after this lol 😴 but just wanted to clarify a few things!:

I was out until 1:15 AM driving my mom and sister to the airport. They are gone this weekend. I discovered I was locked out once I got home. 

Had they been home, I would've just drove straight there. 

I do not have a key to their home. This is the second time they're ever both gone away at the same time, so we've never thought to give me an official key because one of them is always there when I visit.

Why didn't you:

Go to a hotel? 
- I could not have imagined this would take 9 hours. I thought it would be done by 4 AM latest, they also kept promising someone would call. By the time I realized they weren't calling, it wasn't financially worth it, hotels in my area are around $200/night. I'd rather skip one night of sleep honestly, especially if I'm getting there at 5 AM.

Sleep in your parking garage?
- My parking garage is freezing cold, last month there was a break in where things got stolen, I had no blankets, and most of all: there's no cell phone service in an underground parking garage! I also know myself and my sleep habits and it's unlikely I'd even be able to fall asleep.

Stay in your lobby?
We are all different. Some people could've stayed there all night and felt good with that, I stayed in my lobby for 2 hours and couldn't take it anymore. I can't stay stagnant that long. I love driving, I say it all the time, night drives don't scare me, and it's safer to be moving in my opinion. Please also keep in mind I worked 2 AM closing shifts at the movie theater for almost 5 years, and have solo road tripped many states alone at night! While I'm cautious, my town does not scare me.

Thank you to the lovely people who have been so nice and helpful! It was a difficult night, but I'm grateful for you. Also very excited to go to sleep right now. Goodnight everyone ❤️
video_title: locked out of my apartment and no where to go (all nighter vlog)

author: @TheodoraWohler
text:
This just happened to me, I just moved in a couple of weeks ago.  I am not from this area and have no family close by 
I went to the Police Station because I just didn’t know what to do.  They were great!  They called the 24 hour emergency number after I tried several times.  
Interestingly enough the property manager was there within the hour. The police said to the manager, this was a dangerous situation.  If emergency services needed in the building and there was no access, the management company could be held accountable.   
The officer was filing 
an investigation report and a detective from the department would be following up with the management company.  
I was hesitant about going to the police but they said I did the right thing.

author: @debbieb2584
text:
It’s completely unacceptable for a property manager to handle a lockout situation this way. If a tenant had the ability to call a locksmith immediately because management took far too long to respond, then the property manager should absolutely reimburse and compensate them for that expense.

This isn’t just an inconvenience — it’s a serious safety issue. As others have pointed out, what if someone left the stove on, had a pet trapped inside, or there was another emergency situation? Tenants deserve timely assistance and professional communication, especially in situations that directly impact their safety and access to their home.
Glad you’re safe and sound!!

author: @Daphne-ys4ue
text:
Ok!
Must take a breath as I am furious! 😡 
The idea that no one answered that emergency line is absolutely unacceptable! And to have someone who did end up on the line who is 3,000 miles away is ridiculous!
The safety issue is another problem. Luckily you had your car for refuge but what if you didn’t? Where could you have gone where you would have been safe? You could have called the police but what if you didn’t have your phone?! These new apartment complexes charge soooooo much money and then don’t have protocols in place for situations like this!!!
I’m just glad you’re back in there but you never should have been locked out for that long in the middle of the night!  I’m so sorry you had to go through this!! 😢😢😢
video_title: locked out of my apartment and no where to go (all nighter vlog)

author: @nancytownsend8674
text: It’s very easy for people who are not in your situation to say oh you should do this and you should do that they’re not you. You have to do what’s best for you. You’re a grown woman and you know how to handle yourself. You spend your time by yourself and you know yourself very well. I just cannot believe how irresponsible your property management is. I would definitely file a complaint that is unsatisfactory they had no concern for your safety whatsoever. I’m glad you finally got into your apartment personally I think you were pretty smart. Driving around going to 24 hour places. I think it was too funny. You were worried about the guy from McDonald’s singing all the Wendy’s in your car. I personally on Saturdays Starbucks is my big treat and I’ll get a couple of drinks from Starbucks and then I go to Dunkin to get my iced tea and I always think that too is oh they’re gonna see the Starbucks cups in my car. I so enjoy your videos. You are a funny, funny young woman. You have a great sense of self and that is fantastic.
video_title: locked out of my apartment and no where to go (all nighter vlog)

author: @SmooseySmellings
text:
I am a 47 yr old woman who came to follow you many moons ago when my little nephew asked me to follow his favourite youtuber called Pewdiepie that was trying to get 100 million followers. I have watched you since, finding your videos wholesome and uplifting to pass the time. 

I want to say to you that it fills my heart with joy that men and boys of all ages have access to such a wonderful male role model. It's so important and very rare. Thank you for filling that space. And for all young people regardless of gender, you and Marzia have consistently shown up and shared what a healthy relationship looks like. Which is priceless.

Thank you both, for being so epic.
video_title: Ending the vlogs.

author: @Alcatraz-a
text: It’s incredibly rare to find someone who is extremely wealthy yet remains so polite and genuinely sincere. I truly believe PewDiePie is one of those rare individuals. It was such an admirable decision for him to scale back his YouTube career to focus on being a dad, and the fact that he consistently shared that journey with us through his vlogs was amazing. I’ve loved watching them all this time, so I’m honestly a bit sad that we won't get to see them anymore. But looking at it from a family perspective, he absolutely made the right choice. Whether he continues to live in Japan or moves elsewhere, I will always root for him.
video_title: Ending the vlogs.

日本で海外youtuberが迷惑行為等をしているのが頻発してる中で、あまり期待はしていなかったのですが
毎日を楽しそうに、幸せそうに日本での生活や日本を尊重したライフスタイルをしていらっしゃるのがとても素晴らしく日本人としても嬉しかったです。
vlog終了は残念ですが、これからも日本で静かで幸せな暮らしを過ごしていただけたらなと思います。
ありがとうございました。

author: @ryoland1704
text:
あなたが初めて日本旅行の動画をアップした時から、毎回アップされる動画を楽しみにしてきました。
数年前、日本への移住を発表した時は本当に嬉しかったし、コロナでそれが延期になっていることはとても悲しかったです。
日本に移住してから4年間のVlogは本当に素晴らしく、次の動画はいつアップされるか毎日心待ちにしていました。
Vlogをやめてしまうのはとても悲しいですが、移住してから当初の予定から大幅に延長して4年間もアップしてくれたことに感謝しかないです。
長い間楽しませてくれて本当にありがとうございました。これからも日本での素敵な生活を心より祈っています
video_title: Ending the vlogs.

author: @alexamellman8119
text: As a therapist who specializes in trauma (and rape survivor too!) I can't express how PROUD I am of you two. You both articulate the truth of the experience, the devastation of the betrayals that come after from family, friends and sometimes well-meaning but ignorant people trying to help. I was especially happy to hear you both call out how PATHETIC and SMALL the rapists and their enablers are. They get away with it by convincing people they are important and by bullying, and the romanticization of men who have a lot of unattached sex. I thank you also for telling people HOW to support someone who has been raped,  because even after 20+ years of working with survivors, my jaw STILL hits the floor at the dumb shit I hear about what people say who are supposed to be friends. I am so glad you both exist in the world. Thank you for sharing this, thank you for being such brilliant, compassionate, strong women. I wish millions of women see you and learn from you. I wish you all the love and know that you both will not tolerate anything less than the love you give. Wish I could give you a giant mom-hug! THANK YOU AGAIN A MILLION TIMES!
video_title: The Full Story | The Girl Who Died on my Study Abroad Trip & "The Predator of Seville"

author: @analopez7659
text:
Your story resonated deeply with me. I studied abroad in Granada, Spain, and during my first weekend there, I was invited to a club through an agency very similar to Manuel’s. The club was full of foreign female students, all brought there by the same agency.

I only had one drink that night, but shortly after, I felt terribly sick and blacked out. Fortunately, I managed to escape. I was drugged. Months later, a friend I met during the semester — another foreign student who attended the same event — confessed that she had also been drugged that night and r*p*d. Unlike me, she wasn’t able to get away.

Watching this video made me realize that our experience was not an isolated case. These agencies prey on vulnerable foreign female students and take advantage of them. Tragically, this seems to be far more common than most people realize.
video_title: The Full Story | The Girl Who Died on my Study Abroad Trip & "The Predator of Seville"

author: @captainlou9677
text:
"Beware of people who pounce..." it's upsetting to me that we live in a world where women are targeted. I don't have a daughter but I have a sister. 

About 30 years ago I was visiting Los Angeles from living out of state and I went out with my sister and her friends. I was 23 and she was 22 at the time. I was a firefighter and I had friends in law enforcement where I worked as a firefighter. One of these guys had accompanied me to this LA trip because he wanted to see LA. 

About an hour of hanging out at a bar in Pasadena, I noticed my sister was very intoxicated. My sister at the time was known for out drinking people and being in the bar for a short time didn't make sense with how drunk she was. My buddy asked how much she had to drink and it turned out she had only two drinks. The second drink was bought for her by some guy at the bar. I asked her what he looked like and my buddy and I went looking for the guy. My sister had several friends at the bar along with me, my brother, and a few of my friends. Whoever bought her that drink probably realized she was with a lot of people and he left. We never found him. But I really wish we had. 

It makes me mad just thinking about it.

author: @TiffanyCHoyt
text:
The reason they drowned Gabrielle  and other women in forms and paperwork, and then to not acknowledge their claims is to make it more difficult for them at every level to bring this situation to trial. This is sbout institutions not wanting to change. It’s easier for them to put young women from other countries on trial for their behavior than to admit they have a systemic problem. Don’t forget Spain (and Argentina, Siobhan) have been long involved in movements like Opus Dei—organizations purposely designed to suppress young women’s social growth. When you see films like Belén and Soy Nevenka you understand that this is a fight that is still making the claimants bear the burden of proof that they were victims. It’s endemic. 

On another note—I have colleagues who accompany George Washington university students on abroad trips and I have suggested they require all students (female and male) to watch your first video in preparation for the trip.

author: @xXxMEGAMANIAxXxx
text:
I watched the doc after this video. As a survivor myself, my rapist drugged and raped me, everything you said and everything each woman said in the doc left me with such a bitter sweet feeling.  The abuse of innocence and kindness and naivety was a dagger in my heart. Community should be something magical and loving not one borne out of pain and suffering. My rapist got away with it unfortunately, mostly because I couldn’t give a detailed exactly play by play of everything, because his dad was a cop, and because I live in florida. The officer present at while I was getting my kit done told my parents “well it’s Florida and she was drunk so the first rapes free”. The systems in place to protect victims rarely ever work. Somehow the perpetrators end up walking away. People will always take advantage and abuse others. It’s a sad reality of the world we live in. However we as humans create institutions to bring justice. Our institutions are failing us. Globally. How much more do people have to suffer at the hands of monsters?

I now have a wonderful life with an incredible husband who is so kind and patient with me and now have the peace I deserve. But that peace came with hard work  and therapy and many many many sleepless nights. I still flinch when he touches me sometimes. 

Media treats rape as something a person just does and says it’s awful. It’s never looked at as something systemic.

author: @fionamclary7631
text: 40:41 "vague emails and promises of counseling..." Yeah, that is all too real. I'm a grad student at a large public university in the US, and a few weeks ago, an undergrad was murdered in the laundry room of her student apartment. As far as we know, her killer was a stranger to her. Other students reported that he broke into the building previously. The obvious problem here is that the management company of the building (unaffiliated with the university, but it is still student-only housing) repeatedly ignored severe security vulnerabilities in the building that made it possible for a random guy to follow students through the garage and into the laundry room where he killed someone. Residents had complained about broken key card readers for *months*. And all we get from the university are a couple of emails listing the counseling services and saying that this must be especially hard for the LGBTQ community (the victim was trans, although it is still unknown if this played a role in the murder). The student government has provided much more passionate and compassionate communication, but it is so unfair that it is on the community of shocked, scared, and grieving primarily-undergrad students to push the university and this management company to fix the problem that allowed this horrible crime to happen in the first place. So I guess all that to say that I'm sorry to report, institutional communication surrounding the violent or suspicious deaths of students has not improved in the last 10 years. It is still just vague emails and promises (or honestly, merely offers) of counseling.

author: @sevindeeshannon9755
text: Spain, much like England, has a long and horrific history of institutionalizing cruelty towards women. THAT is how we need to regard these situations. It is not the product of a few bad seeds who coincidentally found themselves in each other’s company. A whole travel agency, the entire justice system turning a blind eye to these crimes AGAIN & AGAIN? It’s not coincidence, it’s conditioning. It is a direct result of the violent colonial values that were imposed upon us centuries ago and still haunt us to this very day. We have got to stop turning every abuser into the newest boogeyman & start addressing it for the cultural failing it is. I truly think that is the only way to end, or at least significantly reduce, violence against women.

author: @madgesmic
text: Thank you so much for sharing this story and thank you to Gabrielle for sharing her experience! Your interview with her at the end is such a good demonstration of how law enforcement and prosecutors' offices do NOT work for victims, not in the U.S. and apparently not in Spain either. They don't work at all, for the most part. It's impossible to watch a prosecution play out in court and not be bowled over by the laziness of the prosecutors; they do NO more than they absolutely have to and often not even that, misplacing evidence etc. while cooperating victims do 10,000 X more and often relive hell to do their work for them, unpaid. Prosecutors rely on the courts favoring their side (which they do at least in the U.S.) but it is SHOCKING how little these people do. Like the FBI they are cops working with and for other cops, occasionally being nice to victims ONLY when it's more convenient than brushing them off. I can't imagine having to navigate that system as a victim, much less across countries, so kudos again to Gabrielle.

text: My mom used to make something she called okra no-gooey. You just slice the okra, along with onions, bell pepper, and anything else you wanna mix it with. author: @gik7910
text:
1:47 YES! Surinamese Okra Soup is my favorite soup. It has family members travelling across the Netherlands just to have some of my soup 😂. In my soup I use: 

- beef broth
- beef shank
- salted beef

- okra
- taro leafs
- yellow onion
- garlic
- tomato
- madame jeanette or habanero peper

- pimento balls
- salt
- black peper

- a little bit of sunflower oil
- a big soup pot
- a big pot
- a baking pan
- a cutting bord
- a knife
- garlic crusher (optional)

1. Cook the beef shanks with the beef broth and piment in the big soup pot till the meat is softer (bone should detach from the meat easily, tendernism style). If there is any bone marrow left in the bone throw the marrow (without the bone) back in the pot. Cut the beef into smaller bits (not too small tho it still needs to be around bite size). The now empty beef bones can be discarded. I tend to remove any veins out of the beef but that is not really necessary. Throw the beef back in the pot and add enough water to be considered a soup.

2. While you start the beef shank cook the salted beef in a different pot till there is almost no water left. Throw the water out and rinse the beef out to get rid of the salt.

3. Slice the salted beef in bits. Dice the onion, mince the garlic and dice the tomato. 

4. Put some sun flower oil in a baking pan (its function is to not have the contents stick to the pan, not so much oil that it becomes a deep fry), bake the salted beef a little crispy. Add the onions and garlic. When the onions are shiny add the tomato’s. Cook it a bit and add black peper.

5. Slice the okras (remove the top and the bottom) and the taro leafs (remove the stems). Add them to the pan with the beef shanks.

6. Add the salted beef mixture to the soup.

7. Now either add more beef broth to salt or add some salt to taste. Add more black peper to taste.

TIPS
- Now with the habanero or madame jeanette peper, if you like it hot add it at the start of step one. And leave it in the soup until your aunty snatches it out of the soup and into her soup bowl. If you are not really a fan of the heat add it at step five and remove it after the soup is done.

- I have also made it vegan once. Then skip the meat and add vegetable broth instead. 

- You can control the okra slime by drying the okras with a paper towel prior to slicing it.

- In true Surinamese style: sleeping soup is the best soup as we say. So, the day after making the soup it is often at peak taste. Having your soup sleep does make the salted beef and okra a bit softer than optimal tho. A possibility is adding everything but these 2 items on day one and then the salted beef and okra the next morning. I never do this tho, it is a hassle, plus my family tends to want to clean out that soup pot on day one already. 😂 Just saying, if you want to go Masterchef mode it could probably be done this way the best. Top flavor, crispy salted beef and a little firmer okra than you would have on day 2 of the soup. Just know that if you do this you are being a little extra about it. 


I hope you try it and thank me later 😂.

text:
Dear Kevin,

I've been growing tromboncino squash this year, and it has quickly become my new favourite squash. When it’s young, it works perfectly as a zucchini, but it can also be used as a winter squash once mature. I’ve also found it to be more resistant to certain diseases compared to other varieties.

I think it deserves more attention, and you’d make a great YouTube video about it so more people can discover how versatile it is.
video_title: 16 Crops You Should Plant In June ASAP

Okra idea! 
I blanch and freeze mine then all year we have what I call Okra Fries! No deep frying!

Thaw them, Pat dry, Cut length wise in half. Toss in olive oil salt and pepper or Tony’s Cajun seasoning (my fav) place on a parchment lined baking sheet roast at 425° 25-30 mins till crispy and roasted to your desire. Okra fries! ❤

author: @Donna_G
text:
I've grown okra one time and that was when I was living in Ohio.  I used it in gumbo and in stewed tomatoes.  When I am thinking about what to grow during summer, it's tomatoes.  I ordered seeds from different suppliers and started my seeds at the normal time for me, around the middle of April; but, my seedlings are looking like I just started them two weeks ago. I'll probably end up getting starter plants at the nursery and hope the tiny seedling can catch up once I put them in the garden.

I tried Delicata squash for the first time last summer or fall.  I really like it and decided to safe the seeds and to plant this year.  But, just in case, I also bought a packet of seeds.  One way or another, I'm going to have Delicata squash.

I grow bush beans and have never had them produce all at once and then the plant dies off.  I get beans throughout the summer until the weather starts cooling down and especially after the frost hits.  I have grown Contender, Blue Lake, Kentucky Wonder, Royal Burgundy, and this year am trying a variety called Dragon Tongue.

author: @Zenten040
text:
Lauren, I know you understand how parasocial relationships work, and I completely respect that you don’t owe anyone details about your personal life. But what frustrates me is the mixed messaging saying you want to handle things privately while also using very emotionally charged or clickbait style titles that naturally invite speculation and engagement.

People are obviously going to react and become invested when content is framed that way. It’s not that viewers are entitled to your personal business, but it can sometimes feel like the audience’s concern and curiosity are being encouraged when it benefits the channel, and then dismissed when people respond to it.
I hope you understand this and can somehow understand a few of our perspectives,not that you’re entitled to care,but if you do want to be “authentic and drama free”.

author: @anajen2.0
text: If you can help it, fight like hell for your marriage. Be open to make concessions. Your child’s life will be infinitely better if you’re together in a loving respectful relationship. And this will have incredible impact on your life, and your life will be better. Don’t put “yourself” and your own interests first, instead love others like Christ loved us, he put us above himself. The idea of only seeking your own happiness is futile and the consequences of that in your family will cause you more stress and result in the opposite for you. It’s better to live a simple quiet life in a loving family, then an extravagant wealthy one in a broken family. You guys had a couple really stressful years, lots of changes and moving, new baby, life in public. Don’t let a couple stressful years change a lifetime of what could’ve been. Unless there’s abuse or infidelity, Every hard season passes, try to overcome through the discomfort). (Also 85% of step parents suck, the good ones are very rare). You will never be able to control what next woman Henny brings into Rio’s life, and vise versa, that alone is reason enough to not separate.

text: Continental is usually juice coffee milk. Bakery items such as bagels toast muffins. You may get cereal. Could have a mini fridge with some yogurts in it. Anything like eggs sausage bacon biscuits and gravy those will be considered hot breakfast. Mackenzie you were close on the eggs in a bag. They come in a bag, or a refrigerated carton, but they're put into a pan with butter flavored oil and cooked in a convection oven. The meat items usually come  par-cooked unless it's bacon it's raw and Frozen and then you put them in the oven and cook them. Biscuits are the same, par-cooked Frozen and you put them in the oven and finish them.

author: @Monitschka
text:
The wallpaper, the plants, and the table parade convey the feeling of being in one single, vast jungle. Wonderful.
Incidentally, I, too, grew up with the calls of peacocks. Their cries carry a very long way, after all. Located about a twenty-minute walk from my parents' home in Graz was Schloss Eggenberg, with its free-roaming peacocks. We could hear them all the way at our house. We loved going there—whether we had visitors or simply on a Sunday afternoon. We were overjoyed whenever we found a beautiful feather every now and then. My route to school took me right past it. Whenever I see and hear the Lalande peacocks, those wonderful memories come flooding back. 🦚🦚🦚🦚🦚🦚

text:
It's disgusting how expensive basic human needs can be... I wish you all the best


I find it silly that people are saying this isn't a basic human need...? Like those are her nutrients, her food and water. Humans need that to survive otherwise they'd die. She has a condition that doesn't make her consume things normally which is why she spends this crazy amount of money just to survive. Her nutrients shouldn't be this expensive because she has a health condition that she can't reverse. If someone had her same condition and wasn't fortunate enough to able to afford that then they'd die.
video_title: $1000 Dinner for me + $15 dinner for my family

author: @Caylarion
text:
It's a good thing she keeps putting on hand sanitizer, cause if any unknown germ gets into the supplies that she handles by her hands, that too could be life threatening...

My cousin had to do this too, he used so many steps to keep his supplies clean, and to keep his hands very clean as well, in order to safely deliver his pouches to his stomach without it becoming life threatening...

Everyone's special in their own way, and for this lady to be so strong & happy each day, makes me smile to see that she's doing okay

She's truly an inspiration, and to those who are also suffering from this, whoever you are, don't give up the fight, your strong, kind, and have a heart of gold, cause your worth living for🥰❤️‍🔥💐💐🌹💐

text: Man. I was on Ozempic and it made me so sick but I thought it was worth it to shed weight and get me moving again. author: @sarahm3282
text:
As someone with an autoimmune disease living in the us, its difficult to see these prices as anything but eugenics. 
Our gov could support production costs. They could put limits on prices and just make everything affordable. They could regulate health insurance and prosecute companies that refused to cover lifesaving treatments. 

And they just dont. The money exists. And even if it didn’t, they have the power to put a cap on prices

author: @anabellauren4460
text: Please don’t use Pantene! It makes your hair waxy and greasy. It may make your hair shiny at first but it puts a waxy layer on your hair that weighs it down and makes the strands of your hair stick together. It also contains sulfates and silicones which are very harsh ingredients. So as someone who wants to be a hairstylist it makes me sooo mad when people use Pantene. A good brand that is affordable is Cera Ve!

text: Hi Tiffany. It does seem unfair. I have Stage 4 Breast Cancer. I live in the US. I have commented a few times.  You are the only person I watch and listen to and therefore comment on. It is terrible having to tell people you care about less than good news. I like the part, where you said the radio therapy could eliminate the cancer in your neck. It could. Maybe the new treatment will not be as difficult. Take care Tiffany. Sending all the best thoughts. Reach out if you like. 💙

author: @ItsNikkiBaby44
text: Oh Tiff hun, no matter if there are others are going through the same thing or may even have it worse, that does not make your feelings any less valid. You are still allowed to hurt, grieve, feel overwhelmed, or react however you need to after hearing difficult news and going through what you’re going through. Pain and emotions are not a competition, and your struggles still matter just as much. Please don’t let anyone even yourself make you feel you’re not allowed to feel the way you are. It’s not self absorbed, it’s not selfish it just is what it is and it’s ok! Remember none of this is your fault please don’t blame yourself for putting your mom and Matt through this. They love you unconditionally and would not want you blaming yourself or feeling guilty especially when as you said it’s out of your control. I do understand, I truly do but please be gentle on yourself.  You’re in my thoughts and prayers Tiff! Sending you strength and healing. Hugs! 🫂🤍🙏🏻
video_title: Cancer Has Grown (Results) *Cancer Journey 2026*

author: @katarinasuzannah4245
text: Dear Tiffany, I am so sorry that you are forced to endure this. I have lived with severe mental illness for the last five years after losing my dad to suicide. An important thing I have learned is the healing power of releasing emotion, whether crying, yelling, moving, writing, or whatever helps. For a long time I struggled storing up all my emotions and turning them "off" which manifested in severe anxiety. Having watched your channel for a while I can tell that you are incredibly brave and it is important to you to take care of your family in any way possible. Make sure to allow yourself internally to have some form of release of all the stress, anger, fear, etc that you must carry. Write out all your worst fears and thoughts and burn it. Schedule in crying or screaming into a pillow sessions when you're alone. Also, don't worry about "feeling sorry for yourself". I think it may actually be important to face the injustice of your situation even if temporarily. Perhaps give yourself moments where you just vent all of it out in your head momentarily, like in the shower. Anyway, I am hoping for the best for you, Matt, Amma and your family!

author: @WV3
text: Sweet girl I pray for you daily. I found your channel when my husband was diagnosed with head and neck cancer.  He had to be fitted for a mask as well and had a difficult time dealing with the claustrophobic feel of getting it made and for the first week or so of the radiation. The staff at the cancer center he went to were wonderful and allowed me to stay with him up until they started the radiation until he got comfortable. We applied aquaphor daily to the area where the radiation was directed.  He used Biotene spray and rinse for dry mouth. He used a cool mist vaporizer at night.  And the staff at the cancer center recommended a powder drink called Healios to help prevent mouth sores. My husband declined the feeding tube but regretted it by the end of 7 weeks of radiation because he couldn’t even swallow water and lost weight two weeks after treatment ended.  I feel so invested in your journey now and pray for clear scans after you complete your treatment and for the rest of your long life. May God bless you and your family. 💙
video_title: Cancer Has Grown (Results) *Cancer Journey 2026*

author: @CallieMasters5000
text: I predict you'll have success with your "Nicki Positano IRL" lifestyle travel. Next you'll have to put out products besides your books, maybe some ceramics, tiles, pillow covers, 🍋 soaps or scents. Identify a Tuscany scent to go with the Positano lemon, and get local manufacturers to make them your brand. Martha Stewart started the same way, as a caterer and then into books, products and TV shows. The biggest warning for you though, is to always have multiple backup people to help you in case you suddenly get sick or are otherwise unavailable, including Carlo, Skye, other people from your videos. And I suggest you bring in your dad to do a guest lecture one night, showing off a highlights video of his career projected on a screen outside by the pool, followed by him doing a few stories and answering questions. Maybe like 2O-30 minutes of talking, so it's not too much for him. Also, I wouldn't put too much emphasis on wine on your tours, as many people don't drink alcohol much or at all these days, there are plenty of wine tours out there, and you don't want hungover guests on hour-long hikes! Many visitors to France, Italy and Scotland enjoy seeing a little of the local businesses but don't actually want to drink it. Also, maybe include two options for activities each day: the Indie option that involves hour long walks, and a Holly option that is easier for people that would otherwise be unable to attend your tours.

text:
1:10 I’m so excited!! 😆 and sooo happy for you Nicki!! I know how much you wanted to share Italy and entertain!! 💕 woo 

I’ve been thru some health things (Neuroendocrine cancer surgery that kicked my butt)  and a second marriage & soon divorce from someone I thought I’d be growing old with, and my mom, her car was literally hit by a tree coming from her chemo.  ALL of this happened during COVID as well ….those few years? Changed me! Took away my resiliency that I had been known for!! 
and I was afraid I would just die here alone never to travel again!! Last place I’d been to in Europe was Sweden… 

and after seeing you and your other friends that run the group excursions(also in Tuscany (my brain isn’t remembering their names atm), 

you ALL make me feel like this can happen!! I was scared for the last four years, after all that stuff and that’s how I discovered YT and you all!! And I feel like I’m gaining my MOJO back!! 

Your vlogs etc do really help those of us that may be in a tricky situation!! Thank you for all you share with us!! And congrats again!! Can’t wait to see more adventures 💕 💕

author: @marjones-w6m
text:
Few years ago we dealt with the very similar issues in our house. We live in the middle of nowhere. When we bought our very rustic house we were told there were two septic tanks in our backyard - one for gray and one for black water. When we encountered major issues, we did not know where exactly the tanks were located. Not only we could not find the septic tanks but also there were no plumbers willing to come to work on it. Could not use any water. It was in the middle of winter, too. We were so overwhelmed. We ended up doing it ourselves: we rented ditch witch, laid new lines, and diverted all gray water into the woods. Temporarily. Still not sure where our black water goes. I am afraid that it is only a matter of time before all these issues will be repeated. 

Good luck. At least you live close enough to town that you are able to find septic tank people to help you with the project. And, in the meantime you can live with your parents. We did not have these options.

author: @johncitizen147
text:
Trisodium Phosphate (TSP) and Mold Removal
Effectiveness of TSP
Trisodium phosphate (TSP) is effective in removing mold when mixed with bleach. This combination not only cleans the surface but also kills the mold, making it suitable for preparing areas for repainting or refinishing.

Usage Guidelines
When using TSP for mold removal, follow these guidelines:

Mixing Ratio: Combine TSP with bleach for optimal results. A common ratio is 1 cup of TSP to 3 quarts of warm water, along with 1 quart of bleach.
Application: Apply the mixture using a sponge or brush. Ensure thorough coverage of the affected area.
Safety Precautions:
Wear protective gloves and goggles.
Use long sleeves and pants to minimize skin exposure.
Work in a well-ventilated area to avoid inhaling fumes.

author: @richardross7219
text:
I flatten my cardboard and take it to the town transfer station. At the transfer station is a Swap Shed where there are all kinds freebies(it's where I get my pots). I raise and give away 200+ plants each summer(mostly at my local hospital and my VA hospital).
 Septic systems are often problems at cabins where there were no sanitary inspections. I've seen where the septic tank was a buried old car that collapsed after 30 years. You probably are going to have to put in an entire system. A good contractor should be able to do the entire job in 3 days. Be sure that it is inspected for proper installation. I used to design septic systems and installed the system for our house. A friend was quoted $30,000 for a new septic system 27 years ago. I was able to redesign it for them so that they bought the vacant property next door and the system dropped to $12,000. It is going to take time and money to make the corrections to the cabin. 
Just relax and take each problem as they come. You'll get through this eventually. As far as a mower is concerned, the new stuff is absolute crap. Most are designed to break in under 300 hours. That old Ford tractor that you and your Dad were working on, is a great machine(I bought one for my wife 40 years ago). You probably want something smaller for your place. Look at name brand garden tractors from the 1970s and 1980s. With a mower deck, a plow, and a cart they can be a big help around the place. The old ones are built much stronger than modern and they are easier to work on. Happy Trials. Good Luck, Rick
video_title: This Problem Will Cost Me Thousands

author: @outoftheklosset
text:
Congratulations!!! We line our drawers too and even the top of our bedside tables. I'm disabled and I need water to take my meds and if I spill I know I'm not ruining anything. It's like a thin piece of liner cut to the measurements of the table. Works for us! :) I also use a liner on my dresser where I sometimes put my hair products on so if they leak I'm not ruining my vintage dresser. 

I would recommend getting a smart control for the garage door. This way you use your phone to open it with your password instead of a remote that someone could break into your vehicle and press. I love it! And we also keep a key in a padded lock outside the home. That way if there was an emergency a friend, family member or heaven forbid emergency services can access. I had to use that once when there was an emergency and I was so relieved our door didn't need breaking in.

So happy for you all!!!❤ Very exciting to get chickens! My SIL has chickens and one of them let's me pick her up and hold her. It's the cutest thing! I would recommend following BlackbirdCoop and Purely Chickens. Remember that they like to scratch so sand is very disappointing to them and they'll not be happy!😂

author: @snowcrustracer
text: Funny you mentioned having acidic soil in your covered beds. I was just chatting with a teacher at our local college extension and he was recommending at the end of the season removing the plastic to allow rain and nature to wash away the salts and acids that build up in the soil from all the compost and fertilizers we add during the season. He thinks we intervene to much with our garden soil and all you should add is some fish emulsion to fertilize. He mentioned some study from Columbia University but I could not find any information on his gardening theory.

author: @Toriyota
text:
You can let the goats milk sit at room temperatore until in seperates, then feed the curds to the chickens.  They LOVE IT!!!

Run the whey thru cheese cloth, then  put it in a spray bottle in a 50/50 ratio with either rain water or distilled water. Spray your plants to deter pests and act as a natural fungicide. It's also beneficial to feed nutrients to the soil.

author: @asteria4279
text:
Fruit-forward jams for the win! Most older recipes call for so much sugar because that's what preserved the jam, much like salt in salted meats. With the invention of canning and freezers, that kind of ratio is no longer needed for preservation. 
Too little sugar can result in discoloration over time, but I'm also a fan of more or equal fruit to sugar for the same reasons as you ❤

text: Telluride is great (but expensive). Before driving down there in the summer, though, check the calendar. They have several music festivals there, and it'll be super crowded on those weekends. Also, don't try to drive into Telluride itself. Drive to the nearby "suburb" of Mountain Village, park in the garage, and take the free gondola that goes into Telluride. Telluride is a 19th century mining town, and there is very little parking for visitors.

author: @anacelli
text: The sinus procedure at my job is called nasal release technique it’s an invasive treatment that helps reduce the pressure and helps the equilibrium through the brain. I’ve had it done multiple times for migraines and it helps a lot. Those who have allergies benefit from it as well. It feels like your ears are plugged, sometimes you’ll hear a pop and sometimes it triggers tears or a bloody nose. But personally, I’ve only experienced tears and a runny nose! Definitely worth getting it done☺️ hope it works out well for you

text:
I love the mature beauty eyeliners so much. While it's true that they're probably a little creamy for that waterline, I think the liner focuses on having that creamy, instant pigment, and not causing that skipping that you get with so many other eyeliners. It's really special in this regard, and in the way you can manipulate it for a minute before it sets down. I absolutely love that part. I'm not going to waterline my eyes anyway, as the older we get the more that lid droops and I don't want to bring any attention to it! 😫

Great video Angie, and I always like your honest reviews. I really trust them.

author: @northcarolinagirl
text: All of this, just so patriotic and very American. Family values, good wholesome living, and sweet fellowship is what I wish for everyone. I love hearing the little ones in the background. I like Granny's gifted cross stitch. I have done my fair share over the years and enjoyed it. I'm on a red, white, and blue kick at the moment and have some patriotic things to stitch since America is celebrating her 250th anniversary. I've got that theme going on in my entry, kitchen, and dining area. Since we are remembering those who have passed while serving our country, I'd like to remember my uncle and  great great grandfather who both died in war. My uncle died at Guadalcanal during WW2 and my Great Great Grandfather died serving in the Civil War.

author: @trishsmith552
text: Katie, I am an old woman. In my day (the 60s) we had Home Economics classes in middle school and high school. The metal utensil you used to spread the frosting on the fruit pizza was called a spatula (not the things used to flip food in the pan). I think those were called turners. About the only thing the spatula was used for was to level off measuring cups. In cake making there is a version of that called an offset spatula, used for spreading frosting. That's what you really needed for your pizza. It would keep your knuckles out of the frosting. And I'm with you girl. I can't roll a crust out round to save my life. You should see my flour tortillas lol

text: Your dark chocolate appreciation reminded me of a fond childhood memory. I grew up in my grandma's dance studio, so to speak, that was about 30 minutes away from my home.  .. traveled there a few times a week from the time I was 3 years old. There was a "Mall", this was in the early 60's, near by. About once a month my parents would let me go to a candy store in the Mall , on the way home. I would save up my money, which might be a dollar or two, and ask to buy a chunk of dark chocolate for that amount. 🥰 And I would eat and savor that "chunk" of dark chocolate until my next chance to visit that store again. 😊 Thank you for bringing that precious memory back to me. 🤗❤️❤️❤️

author: @AnneQ-bk6wi
text:
Fruit pizza is what we call that dessert.  We like to add some canned mandarins, pineapple and when we have fresh raspberries, they go on too.
When the cookie dough sticks I use a dusting of icing sugar instead of flour to keep it from sticking.  That way the cookie dough doesn’t absorb too much flour.
Corrie if you want to crossstitch there are lots of little kits that you could get a small simple project to see if you enjoy it.  My mom would get stamped crossstitch kits because she didn’t like all the counting in the counted cross stitch.  They still turned out beautiful.  I just gave the last baby blanket she cross stitched to one of her grandsons as a gift for his first baby.

author: @AliciaTankersley-x5i
text: Corie I’ve crossstiched on and off for as long as I can remember ( I’m now 86). My last project was quite the endeavor for me. My great grandson was born January 2024, and I made 40 x 60 crib quilt that was Noah’s ark and animals. If you have a fabric store near by, check there for printed patterns. The pattern will give you the color thread needed, and corresponding color number. I suggest starting with simple designs. Maybe Christmas Mason jar lid covers, Christmas ornaments, etc. Those would not be very costly projects, and they are adorable. I believe my cost for the thread for the quilt was $125, I don’t recall the pattern price, then I had it professionally machine quilted. Cross stitch pillow covers are another easy beginner option.

author: @PattyWilliams-bw6bj
text: Hon, if I may call you hon, as a woman who has been in the green industry for 30 years ( not climate change green) but the plant and tree industry there is a newer system for watering. It’s called “ tech line”. The irrigation done underground rather than just an above ground drip system. The tech line has the emitters inside of the tubing. It’s placed under the soil at the plant or tree roots, then covered with soil and mulch. There is absolutely no water waste. We have been using it and it’s saved money on water and plant replacement. We can choose emitter spacing 6” apart on up to specific water needs. The line is used in a circular pattern secured with 4” landscape staples pushed gently onto the tech line. It’s an initial investment but the cost is minimal compared to water loss and plant or tree loss. I’m not a sales rep just someone who has experienced drought , overhead water loss issues and freezing temps that can destroy entire water systems. Blessings!
video_title: Are you paying attention and preparing accordingly? #farmblog #gardening

text: Agree 💯👍. Thank you for your wisdom shared here. I use a Berkey water filter system, but use Dulton ceramic candle filters. It filters about 3 gallons with 1 fill, but can be filled up again as it distills. I also keep about 5 gallons of tap water on hand for misc use. I used to live in the country and could grow food. I am stocking up and canning. I have minimal balcony space now but am growing micro greens. They are easy to handle, grow and are 10 times the nutrient value. Your strawberries are beautiful. text: Welcome home.  THAT IS A WONDERFUL VULTURE. The story goes like this-In all the animal world the vulture was the most beautiful bird to exist. When the sun came too close to the earth, the creatures had to send someone to push away the sun. The giraffes tried, but they were not tall enough and after several others failed at the task, the most beautiful bird, the vulture, took it on. When the vulture returned, it was burned and its loveliness was no more, but the sun was no longer a threat to the creatures of the earth. And that is how the vulture came to its present state. Thank you for all you share here. Always a pleasure. Take care.

author: @karimahoneylifeadventures
text:
I like to believe that our dads are still with us, somehow. 💛 I lost my dad 7 years ago. He loved his cheap black watch! 😳 Pretty silly. I always tried to figure out how to set it for him at daylight savings! When he was passing, he kept catching the watch on the sheets. So we took it off him.
😢 Days later, I heard this beeping, his watch had alarms set for his meds. I kept his watch. 💜 I love it!
It still beeps....and I say..."Hi dad." 😭
I'm so grateful for memories...and tears! 💙🧡💜💚🩷
video_title: It's Time to go Home | Land Rover Defender Truck Camping

text:
Ufff the "how are you doing" question...
To be completely honest: I've been really STRESSED lately. I'm about to write my bachelor's thesis, and I have been procrastinating it because starting it means finishing my studies, and I don't know if I am ready for that. Anxiety and the uncertainty of the future are things I've been struggling a lot more lately. 
And your videos have been helping me so much! Your mantra: "Be where your feet are" has grounded me so many times, as well as your good energy 🩷🫂 thank you so much for that!
Also 6:45 you guys are GLOWING 😍 being with your person really makes you shine 💋

author: @kjellurie
text:
to answer the question: I’m doing well—for the most part 😅

I moved to a new city almost 6 months ago—I’m still adjusting to living independently. I love exploring and trying new cafes here but honestly, there are times I feel so alone. I find it hard to make friends here, even at work. Since I’m the sole member of my team that works in the province here in the 🇵🇭 (they all work in the metro), it’s harder to relate with my other co workers that aren’t in my team here.

But I’ll be traveling to see my team for the first time this week!! I’ll also be catching up with my high school besties who are also living in the metro so that’s motivating me to get through this week 😂🎉

author: @CosmicDuskWolf
text: The one thing and maybe Jacque can try this (since he loves tomatoes) that I have had great success with tomatoes is planting the tomatoes closer together and they have a tendency to hold each other up. Especially with cherry tomatoes. Though I have found if ignored tomatoes tend to take over what ever ground I plant them in, so I do what my grandpa does and use containers. From everything I heard from people, this seems a little close to grow so many different kinds of peppers together. I grew up in the bay area, and I rarely remember having a problem with powdery mildew. Of course my grandpa taught me how to prune when the leaves looked bad, so maybe I did and didn't know what it was that I was pruning.

author: @Распутин-и5х
text: I follow a lot of different gardening creators from all over the world on youtube and one thing I always find fascinating is how different the issues people have in the garden are. You are blessed with a sublime climate for growing most things, but you have issues I will never have to battle with. In my 20+ years of gardening I have never seen a tomato with mildew. Cucumbers, squash and melons on the other hand are basically dead on arrival if the weather aint right. In your mostly dry climate the slug pressure likely isnt too great. If I mulched everything like you do, Id have nothing left within 1 month because the slugs would be throwing a party. Also, because of the rain here in central europe you can count yourself lucky if your tomatoes out in the open dont have blight by august. We have to have em in greenhouses or at least build a roof structure of sorts and even then its no guarantee you get spared. The florida weave works here too but you have to space out your tomatoes way more and reduce leaf mass or its an open invitation to fungi and youll have nothing but brown slop by mid july. Whenever I see you and Kevin have great harvests in april and march(a time I cant even start to plant out most things yet) I always wish I had your climate here. But then I see all your issues I dont have to battle with and realize, I dont have it all that bad after all. Im in something like a zone 8 equivalent here. Continental climate.

author: @TonyTheTerrible
text:
theres a noticeable difference in overall plant production when you pick breaker or ripe tomatoes. i see this all the time with my "super san marzano" tomatoes which have a thick skin and can stay on the plant for WEEKS while ripe. the next fruits dont even begin to ripen until the ripe ones are picked

also on the yellow leaves affected by powdery mildew: some tomatoes are resistant to PM, like the super san marzano that i have. what resistant means is that they still manage to function even with yellowy infected leaves. the plant i mentioned above looks like its on its last leg but it continues to support its tomatoes.

text: Idk if you keep your bread in your cabinet or the fridge but when I first moved out I always kept my bread on the counter/cabinet and it molded SO fast. I was always confused and frustrated bc at home I never remembered bread molding and I was talking with my coworker about it and she told me to always store bread in the fridge. As in like it was common sense and everyone did it 😂 idk why my family never did, I guess we went through bread fast enough to where it never molded but as a household of just 2, bread in the fridge last MUCH longer. Like three times longer and I’m not wasting it throwing it out after a week now. I know this is random but I noticed it and remembered dealing with that 😂

author: @AlconburyBlues
text: I have several Anker power banks. Most of them over a year old, and none of them have died or caused power surges. But I would never buy one with pre-attached cables. That's a poor design. If the cable goes bad, it renders the entire power bank useless. It's much cheaper to buy a standard Anker power bank, and have on hand, several separate cables, and just keep them in the bag that comes with the power bank.

Your issue reminds me more of the zhiyun x100 portable video light... the battery on that apparently sent a reverse charge into anything I plugged it into, frying my motherboard, then 2 chargers before I realized it was the battery, not anything I was trying to charge it with. 

With all the mystery factories making these parts, I'm willing to bet it's the same circuitry issue, so not the charging, but reverse voltage out of the battery.

text:
Questions for your next video:
1) Do you have a video editor or do you do it all yourself?

2) If you’re using a video editor, then how do you send over your footage on location…uploading must be expensive and take ages?

10) If you’re missing b-roll do you re-film or go to a stock website…if the latter, then which one do you use?

author: @al1ce_rabb1t
text: So, my best guess for the Anker powerbank and cable is that the break in the cable caused a voltage spike somewhere and in your and Luke's laptops fried the PowerDelivery controller(s) in your MacBooks. When no PD negotiation takes place, for any charger+device combo with USB-C, it'd function the same as charging over USB-A, on 5V and about 2 or 3A at most, which means 10-15W at best. For a MacBook Neo or M1 Air, that's kind of enough for slow but regular charging. But for anything more power hungry, like 14 or 16 inch MacBook Pro's, that only amounts to trickle charging. It sounds like in the case of your MacBook in particular, it damaged more than just the PD controller, but probably also further power management inhibiting the laptop from engaging your performance cores and GPU cores or simply power-throttling them severely.
video_title: I’m Leaving London Again, But I Need To Explain Why

author: @ChrisArmstrong-ir3fg
text:
G’day Jasmin,
 Well done, very, very tight.😊
 Whilst researching for my container build, I also looked at the spray foam. I also found the here in OZ that it was so bloody more expensive to do, compared to the US. 
 So my plan changed to painting the inside walls with ‘kill rust’ then install foam boards. I’ve painted kill rust under my cars, previously and that prevented any rust ever coming through.
 I did find during my research, that the containers due to the weather (on the inside) have sweat and condensation issues.
 Anyway have a good one,
 Cheers mouse

author: @bellsdawne
text: So I am in dental hygiene school and one of my teachers is a myofunctional therapist. The claim that expanding your palate improves health isn’t completely false; however, everything I’ve been taught has shown promise in GROWING palates. If you use a removal palate widener from the ages 18m-12 years, it helps widen your airway and reduce the risk of developing sleep disordered breathing as an adult. BUT these are new studies and in adults it’s not the same bc the bones in your palate are fused. Orthodontia has a reputation of being much more painful and extensive in adults because the bone is more dense. I don’t know if this matters at all but it’s weird to see how they turned an emerging preventative therapy into red-pill content😢

The sutures in the skull harden by about age 14-16 and trying to expand the palate at that point is a pain in the ass.

author: @JayeCole
text:
FYI / clarification - GCSEs are not like the SATs and do not get you entrance into a university.

We have a different school system and secondary schools here in the UK are for ages 11-16 and end in exams which give you a “qualification”. Typically pupils will want at least 7 but sometimes they will get as many as 9 GCSE qualifications. 

From 16-18 students attend Sixth Form College which ends with exams called A-levels. These are more specialised and most students will get 3 and sometimes 4 of these qualifications. These are used to apply for university.

So her kids are not only not getting the equivalent of a high school diploma, but they aren’t even getting a middle school-ish level of education.

Without GCSEs it will be nearly impossible to get employment anywhere.

author: @KimOpperman
text:
You can go back and look at your Walmart order online and figure out what brand soil that was that was contaminated. I would definitely report this because they really need to do a recall on the soil. 

Back a couple years ago right around 2020-2022 ish- they had a huge problem with potting soil that was contaminated with all kinds of items that should never have been in soil. 

There was a huge investigation. They had determined that they were finding composted, plastic, diapers, and all kinds of junk. 

I remember my garden doing horrible for a couple years after having used that soil. Everything was dying or getting viruses. 

That was also the same time period that random packets of seeds were arriving in online orders and being sent out to people from Amazon,  that were also contaminated. 

They were coming in from certain foreign countries in East Asia, and probably intended as biowarfare.

Ohio Department of agriculture was investigating all of that, and asked  to send samples and seeds that they have not ordered.    

It’s crazy scary that we have to worry about dirt and garden seeds,  whether or not someone has tampered  with them.  

It makes praying  over our food,  garden,  and family all the more important.

author: @catracampolieto8989
text: In 2020 during "C" I took two months off from work. The company was essential. I tried to grow food, I knew nothing. In the fall of that year I built raised beds, added soil and manure. I learned how to waterbath and pressure can. If I can grow and preserve anyone can. Have a good day.

author: @lynnboyett5540
text:
Why am I gardening?
When I was thirteen, I listened to my grandfather talk about the Depression. He was a prolific gardener with a large, bountiful garden every year. I realized the cycle of circumstances and that I would probably face a “depression” in my lifetime. I’ve prepared.
Also, I’m been financially wiped out, to zero, three times for different reasons each time. I have the experience of knowing that all of it can happen to me even with the best of plans.
Now, the prices in my retirement years are rising very fast for a fixed income. I’m glad I’ve invested time in learning gardening early because the learning curve would have put me behind otherwise.
Look, if I can learn pressure canning at 64, anyone can.
video_title: Food Prices Are About to Surge… Start Growing NOW

author: @judymadge7347
text: I recently moved house.  We have 3 veg patches that hadn't been used in years and were desperate for nutrients! I've turned the soil, fertilised and sown some veg even here in Melbourne!  I got a worm farm for Mother's Day and am setting up more composting areas.  I'm watching the sun and shade to determine what to plant where, but, in the meantime, I've planted some peas, garlic, potatoes, broccoli, onions, lettuce and some flowers.  I'm growing my own veg as I question the nutritional value of today's veg in the supermarkets and I also do not want to pay such ridiculous prices!  And finally, I find a deep sense of peace when I'm growing my own food!  Dirty fingernails = my happy place!
video_title: Food Prices Are About to Surge… Start Growing NOW

author: @chandrapate9602
text: Where I live in the US I can start growing in early spring to fall. Where my home is I am not allowed to grow food in the ground, so I went to Lowes ,and bought food grade buckets. This all started when i went to the grocery store and the price of roma tomatoes was 3.60 a pound, 1 bell peppers was 1.27 . I am not paying that so I started growing my own.

author: @spudsmckenzie4123
text:
My recommendations for super easy and plentiful crops that you can grow in grow bags or small troughs would be things like the following which require little effort from you and have good disease and pest resistance;

Cherry tomatoes
Silverbeet (also known as chard)
Carrots
Drawf beans
Onions
Potatoes
Garlic
Jerusalem artichoke

Also herbs like Parsley, Basil, Rosemary and Thyme are so simple to grow that anybody can get them going. All of these grow just about anywhere and can be done with minimal effort. And for those starting out, a worm farm is a weapon to making your own liquid fertiliser and castings for enriching your soil. Just don't feed them meat, any onions or citrus. They don't like those items but all other veggie and fruit scraps get turned into liquid gold very quickly.

author: @Aydeet2
text:
I am 71, a retired educator, and happily single and independent.  I so enjoy your channel, Jo, and I thank you for sharing your adventures, clothing, shoes, (whether you were gifted something or purchased on your own...it doesn't matter). The effort you put forth in creating your vlogs provides so much entertainment and fun! I love to see your beautiful outfits, jewelry, etc.! 
    Ladies, don't bemoan someone who can do something that you can't or for which you lack the funds to afford. Don't expect others to deny what they love just because you are unable. Find your path all on your own. Think for yourself, take the time to create what you CAN do. You will be amazed at your findings! Maybe say a little thank you to Jo for the enormous preparation that goes into making  these vlogs😊 Again, from me,thanks, Jo, for all you do!

author: @CBEEBLE
text:
What happened to the planters/furniture (assuming paid for with communal funds) on the roof terrace? All tenants/residents had keys which strongly suggests that access was not accidental or unauthorised. Even if the lease does not expressly grant access, the terrace appears to have been treated as an established amenity of the building rather than a private or restricted area.
You should write collectively to the managing agent and ask them to confirm who authorised the change of locks, what happened to the planters/furniture and the basis relied upon for withdrawing access from residents and leaseholders without consultation. They should also be asked to confirm whether the withdrawal of access is connected in any way to any proposed roof, airspace or upward development, or any intention by the freeholder to preserve or assert development rights over the roof area.
Unfortunately, if the lease does not explicitly grant roof access and access has merely been permitted historically, the position may be less straightforward. However, the fact that residents were given keys and the terrace appears to have been used as a shared amenity means it is entirely reasonable to ask for a clear explanation before accepting that access can simply be withdrawn.

author: @Jeanelleats
text:
The US is a huge place, does your movie theater offer anything different from ours?
When we had movie theater food in Hawaii, we got green beans. In Japan, we got a white chocolate churro.
I may take what we have at home for granted but I’m sure there are people out there who are curious to see the snacks we have here 🍿
👩🏻‍🍳 Recipe blog: jeanelleats.com
PS I was telling my parents I wanted a Grogu plushie while they were throwing them out and immediately after I was hit in the face with one 😂 ASK AND YOU SHALL RECEIVE

author: @taylorgayhart9497
text: We have a range in the US, some small/locally owned theaters still exist and it’s just popcorn and candy. AMC is pretty standard, most theaters have a bar now but the food is still what you saw here, pizza, nachos, and popcorn. Then there are full service theaters that have a full menu where you order a meal at the beginning, and it’s served in the first 15 minutes of the showing. Cinebistro is my favorite, they have a great salmon entree.

author: @lqstar
text: There are several movie theaters near me that have restaurants inside them. Each section has their own wait staff and each chair has a button to call for service or you can use the QR to order to your seat. The price of the movie and food aren’t any more expensive than other places. The food tastes good and often has themed food and drinks for the bigger movies. Some of the theaters have regular seating with tables on the sides and others have more private seating like pods with chairs and larger tables. The pod seating often has blankets as well.

author: @lisanoble7686
text: MOLLY!! 3 years ago I lost my hair due to low iron (I basically had NONE because of chronic illness). I’d give anything to be able to show you the before and afters! To help with my confidence I SWORE BY the clip in pony’s from beauty works which have now been discontinued and it’s broke my heart 😭😭 they saved me. So I could wear that out but anytime I was at home there was no tension on my hair. Get your iron back up after baby is born, silk scrunchies and pillowcase, hair oil, always blow dry, no air dry! Don’t brush too much when wet and invest in a kind brush for wet hair. ❤ the best time to do it is post partum when you will be doing less of the stuff you usually need extentions for xxx

یہ ایک پاکستانی موٹر سائیکل ٹریول بلاگر ہیں جو اصل میں جاپان جانا چاہتے تھے، لیکن روس اور یوکرین کی جنگ شروع ہونے کے بعد جاپان نے روس سے جاپان جانے والے راستے بند کر دیے، اس لیے انہیں اپنا راستہ بدلنا پڑا اور وہ چین کے راستے سفر کرتے ہوئے آگے بڑھے۔

انہوں نے اپنے سفر میں افغانستان، کرغزستان، ترکمانستان، روس، منگولیا اور چین سے گزر کر، پھر تھائی لینڈ کے ذریعے اپنی موٹر سائیکل جاپان پہنچائی۔
